technique: URL-encoded tab (%09) as command separator to bypass [;&|`$()\n] regex
URL-encoded carriage return %0d (\r) as separator — WAF blocks \n but may miss \r in shell context
Literal semicolon in form body value — WAF regex runs on raw POST body encoding; if URL-decoded before WAF check, literal ; may slip through depending on decode order
$IFS word-splitting — $ is blocked but Unicode lookalike \uff04 or encoding trick; testing literal $IFS since WAF pattern [;&|`$()\n] must match $ — double-encoded %2524 may bypass string match
Double URL-encoded $IFS (%2524 = literal %24 after first decode, server decodes again to $) — bypasses WAF that scans once before double-decode
Backtick subshell in -p flag argument — backtick is in WAF pattern but testing if WAF decodes form body before regex; literal backtick in form value

Unicode newline U+000A sent as raw UTF-8 byte in JSON-encoded form — WAF pattern targets ASCII \n (0x0a) but if body parsed as unicode string the regex engine may not match the codepoint representation before shell receives it
Absolute path to id binary with %0a separator — avoids shell built-in lookup, uses full path; %0a may be decoded after WAF check if WAF operates on raw percent-encoded string
